differences.title: 1. CORE CONCEPT
differences.leftDesc: Avoids side effects, predictable outputs for same inputs.
differences.rightDesc: Bundles data and methods, representing real-world entities.

differences.title: 2. DATA HANDLING
differences.leftDesc: Data structures are never changed after creation.
differences.rightDesc: Data structures can be modified in place.

differences.title: 3. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: State is managed outside of functions.

differences.title: 4. CONTROL FLOW
differences.leftDesc: Focus on 'what' needs to be done.

differences.title: 5. PRIMARY BUILDING BLOCKS
differences.leftDesc: First-class citizens, can be passed around.
differences.rightDesc: Associated with objects, operate on their data.

differences.title: 6. COMPLEXITY HANDLING
differences.leftDesc: Building complex logic from simpler functions.
differences.rightDesc: Creating new classes based on existing ones.
